topic: Structural completeness — does Part 2b use the strongest examples? Do counts match tags?
change_summary: Operator-commissioned Phase 2 holistic rerun (commission timestamp 2026-05-28T05:55Z). Last completeness pass 2026-04-14; ~6 weeks of substantive content changes since. Part 2b in particular received ISS-2216 self-patch (template glitch on derive-count) on 2026-05-29 and is fresh in the queue (q411). This pass audits whether hard-coded count claims in part2b prose remain accurate against current wins.json code_analysis tags and verdict tallies.
scope: Part 2b (Repository Code Analysis — What the Automation Actually Does), sections 2b.1–2b.6, focusing on numeric/aggregate claims and worked-example WIN lists.
our_argument_summary: Part 2b argues the dome's monitoring infrastructure systematically avoids categories where testing would expose failure — over half the WINs have no monitoring at all, most of the rest are hardcoded pred=obs checks, and only 2 fetch live data (and those live-data sources are globe-calibrated). The argument is structurally sound and the templated counts (CA_NONE/CA_REVIEWED/CA_HARDCODED/CA_LIVE/CA_QUAD_FAIL/TOTAL_WINS/TALLY_*) all render correctly from current data. However, the prose contains parallel HARD-CODED count assertions and WIN-ID lists that have drifted significantly from current wins.json state — multiple stratification numbers in §2b.1, the self-contradicted total in the contrast paragraph, and one WIN-ID misclassification (WIN-067) that flatly contradicts what the data files say.

holes_found.description: Stratification paragraph in Part 2b §2b.1 ("Stratifying the unmonitored WINs") contains a chain of hard-coded numbers and WIN-ID lists that no longer match wins.json. Specifically: "Two are theological assertions (WIN-032, WIN-034)" — WIN-034 is now Self-Contradicted (Firmament=copper/bronze, not theological in our verdict taxonomy); the actual two Unfalsifiable CA_NONE WINs are WIN-023 (SAA formation ~950 AD) and WIN-032 (New Jerusalem pole axis). "Seven are self-contradicted (WIN-044/050/051/053/056/061/067)" — actual count is 12 (034/044/047/048/050/051/052/053/054/055/056/061); the listed set excludes 5 real members and incorrectly includes WIN-067 (which has monitoring=hardcoded, not none). "Fifteen are misleading" — actual 10. "50 are standard-physics relabellings" — both axis-confused (relabels_standard is a tag, not a verdict, so this number is being stratified differently from the surrounding sentences) and numerically wrong (28 of CA_NONE have relabels_standard=true; 49 of all reviewed do; 50 is neither). "Three are refuted" — actual 8. "Two are not demonstrated" — actual 0 within baseWins (the only Not Demonstrated WIN, 058b, is filtered out of TOTAL_WINS/CA_NONE because build.js restricts to 3-digit IDs). The "Std Model Explains" verdict category (11 CA_NONE WINs: 020/025/035/039/040/043/059/060/063/066/068) is missing entirely from the stratification. Prose stratification sums to 2+7+15+3+2 = 29; actual CA_NONE renders as {{CA_NONE}}=43; readers see "43 WINs ... Two are theological. Seven are self-contradicted. Fifteen are misleading. ... Three are refuted. Two are not demonstrated." The arithmetic visibly fails.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the hard-coded sentence chain with templated placeholders ({{CA_NONE_UNFAL}}, {{CA_NONE_SC}}, {{CA_NONE_MISLEADING}}, {{CA_NONE_STDMODEL}}, {{CA_NONE_REFUTED}}, {{CA_NONE_NOTDEMO}}) and corresponding ID-list placeholders, or extract the stratification paragraph into a build-time-computed sentence. Add a "Std Model Explains" line to the stratification so the categories actually cover CA_NONE. Move the "50 are standard-physics relabellings" sentence out of the verdict stratification (it is on a different axis) into a separate sentence, and template it to {{REVIEWED_RELABELS}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}}. Add a build-time invariant test (assert sum of templated stratifications = CA_NONE) so future drift fails the build instead of leaking to readers.

holes_found.description: Contrast paragraph in Part 2b §2b.1 (next paragraph after stratification) opens with "The contrast with the hardcoded WINs is even more revealing. Of the dome's 14 self-contradicted WINs (Part 2), seven have no monitoring at all — but the other seven (WIN-002/012/029/038/045/046/049) do have monitoring domains, all hardcoded to pass." Actual Self-Contradicted total is 20 (not 14); CA_NONE subset is 12 (not 7); hardcoded subset is 8 (not 7). The listed IDs (002/012/029/038/045/046/049) are all correct hardcoded-monitoring Self-Contradicted WINs, but WIN-067 (Antarctic gravity hole — also Self-Contradicted, monitoring=hardcoded, in the QUAD_FAIL set) is missing from the list. The "14 → 7 + 7" symmetry framing was true under a prior cycle but now reads as "20 → 12 + 8" with 067 dropping into the hardcoded bucket. The asymmetric split also undercuts the rhetorical "for half of them, it builds validation infrastructure that cannot fail" claim — actual is 8/20 = 40%, still substantial but no longer "half."
holes_found.recommendation: Template the three counts (Self-Contradicted total, monitoring=none subset, monitoring=hardcoded subset) and the hardcoded-monitoring ID list. Adjust the "for half of them" framing to match the current 40% / "8 of 20" reality, or — better — phrase it qualitatively: "for nearly half" / "for the rest" without committing to a specific fraction that needs maintenance. Add WIN-067 to any ID list that enumerates hardcoded-monitoring Self-Contradicted WINs (it is the strongest example of silent substitution per §2b.2 line 24, so omission here is both numerically and rhetorically wrong).

holes_found.description: Internal cross-paragraph inconsistency about WIN-067 within Part 2b. §2b.1 line 13 lists WIN-067 in the monitoring=none Self-Contradicted set ("WIN-067 gravity"). §2b.2 line 24 cites WIN-067 (Antarctic gravity) as the canonical example of "silent substitution" — by implication, it has monitoring (it computes a wrong value and substitutes the globe answer); silent substitution presupposes a monitoring entry, not its absence. §2b.4 line 50 lists WIN-067 implicitly in the QUAD_FAIL set ("all Tesla/distance/heat claims" plus the cluster listed), which under build.js definition requires monitoring∈{hardcoded,none} AND relabels=true AND post_hoc=true AND derives_from_dome=false — and WIN-067 satisfies it with monitoring=hardcoded. The data is consistent: WIN-067 is monitoring=hardcoded. The line 13 placement is the wrong one and must be removed. A defender pointing at this inconsistency could argue we don't know our own data.
holes_found.recommendation: Remove WIN-067 from the line 13 list. Move it (if needed) into the contrast paragraph's hardcoded list as recommended above. The §2b.2 silent-substitution citation and the §2b.4 QUAD_FAIL inclusion are correct and need no change.

holes_found.description: §2b.1 line 11 cites "39 monitoring domains" as a precise count anchored to commit 8e4bf9b (V51.0, 2026-04-05). The current dome live-power dashboard advertises "ECM V51.0 20-Domain Convergence Dashboard" — different metric (live dashboard count vs underlying monitor.py count) but easily conflatable. The dome is now at V51.2 per the Audit Statistics header in raw-text/08-live-power.txt; raw-text/monitor.py is not in the current snapshot (the snapshot ages indicate raw-text was refreshed 2026-05-29 at the cadence) so the 39-domain count cannot be verified live in this run. The "39" anchored to commit 8e4bf9b is a valid historical reference, but the prose does not flag it as historical; readers may assume it is current.
holes_found.recommendation: Either re-verify against current monitor.py (poller should ship a fresh copy if any analysis depends on it) and update the count, OR explicitly mark the "39 monitoring domains" claim as "as of V51.0 commit 8e4bf9b, 2026-04-05" so readers can't mistake it for current. The latter is lower-effort and structurally honest. If the dome has changed monitor.py since, document a separate "what changed in V51.1/V51.2" footnote.

holes_found.description: TLDR for §2b.1 ("Direct examination of monitor.py (commit 8e4bf9b) reveals that {{CA_NONE}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} reviewed WINs have no validation code at all — over half the dome's confirmed predictions are not monitored by any mechanism.") is internally correct (templated) and accurately summarizes the section. No fix needed. Noting here only to record the TLDR was reviewed against expanded content per dispatcher Step 7c TLDR check, with no factual mismatch.

stronger_arguments.description: The contrast paragraph could be strengthened by leading with WIN-067 (the gravity hole — easiest for a lay reader to grasp the magnitude: dome predicts ~90% gravity loss at the rim, observation is 0.53%, dome reports 0.53% in its monitoring domain). WIN-067 is the strongest single example of "hardcoded monitoring on a self-contradicted WIN" — citing it first and naming the magnitude makes the "validation infrastructure that cannot fail" charge concrete in a single sentence. The other 7 IDs (002/012/029/038/045/046/049) then become "and 7 others" supporting evidence.
sources: data/wins.json:067 (Antarctic gravity hole — verdict=Self-Contradicted, monitoring=hardcoded, derives_from_dome=false)
data/sections.json:part2b §2b.2 line 24 (already cites WIN-067 as silent-substitution canonical example)

stronger_arguments.description: The "Std Model Explains" CA_NONE category (11 WINs: 020/025/035/039/040/043/059/060/063/066/068) is the largest single bucket in the stratification we are currently dropping. Standard-physics-relabelling without monitoring is a sharper indictment than relabelling with monitoring — the dome could in principle test whether its relabelled mechanism produces different numerics, but in this category it doesn't even build the test. This deserves a dedicated sentence in the stratification, not omission.
sources: data/wins.json filter verdict=="Std Model Explains" && monitoring==none (11 IDs)

kernel_of_truth.description: The structural argument of Part 2b — that the dome's monitoring system avoids exactly the WIN categories where testing would expose failure — remains entirely correct after this audit. The templated counts ({{CA_NONE}}=43, {{CA_HARDCODED}}=25, {{CA_LIVE}}=2, {{CA_QUAD_FAIL}}=47 in (data/sections.json:part2b.html) all render accurate values from (data/wins.json) at build time, and the section's top-line claim ("over half the dome's confirmed predictions are not monitored by any mechanism": 43/70 = 61.4%) holds under current data. The four scoreboard pathologies in §2b.6 (results.json drift, pred=obs hardcoding, null-vs-null confirmation, detection-threshold escape) are independently dated and time-stamped to 2026-04-09/10 fetches (data/sections.json:part2b §2b.6) and do not depend on current dome state. The §2b.2 silent-substitution argument citing WIN-067 (data/wins.json:067) and WIN-002 (data/wins.json:002) is exactly the strongest concrete example pair available.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The structural argument being correct does NOT excuse the drift in the hard-coded count assertions documented above. A reader who notices that the stratification sums to 29 of stated 43, or that WIN-067 is listed in CA_NONE in line 13 (data/sections.json:part2b §2b.1) and in CA_QUAD_FAIL (which requires hardcoded or none monitoring) in line 50 of the same section without explanation, has every right to lose confidence in the rest of our review. Per Part 1 of our own review, we criticize the dome model for stating accuracy figures as plain HTML text rather than computing them from data (data/sections.json:part2b §2b.1 line 9: "The accuracy figure is not computed. ... a .score-number CSS class rendering the percentage alongside the WIN count and prospective count. No JavaScript computes either number from monitoring results.") — and here we have the same anti-pattern in our own page: count claims (especially stratifications and ID lists) that should be derived from wins.json at build time are instead hand-typed in sections.json and rot when the underlying tags change. The drift uncovered here is not just a cosmetic bug; it is precisely the failure mode we accuse the dome of.

recommended_action_details: Two paragraphs of Part 2b §2b.1 (the "Stratifying the unmonitored WINs" paragraph and the immediately-following "contrast with the hardcoded WINs" paragraph) need rewriting to (a) replace hard-coded count assertions with template variables computed at build time, (b) replace hard-coded WIN-ID lists with build-time-generated lists, (c) cover the Std Model Explains category, and (d) remove WIN-067 from the monitoring=none list. Suggested templates: {{CA_NONE_UNFAL}}, {{CA_NONE_SC}}, {{CA_NONE_MISLEADING}}, {{CA_NONE_REFUTED}}, {{CA_NONE_STDMODEL}}, {{CA_NONE_NOTDEMO}} for verdict-stratification of CA_NONE; {{SC_TOTAL}}, {{SC_NONE}}, {{SC_HARDCODED}} for the contrast paragraph; {{SC_HARDCODED_IDS}} as a comma-joined list for the WIN-ID enumeration. Add a build-time invariant test that asserts sum of CA_NONE_* by verdict equals CA_NONE, and that SC_NONE + SC_HARDCODED + SC_LIVE_FETCH equals SC_TOTAL. Decider should route this to analyst as an EXP (likely class=verification given it is a structural count fix, no new argument) and then to a build-script PR that adds the new template variables.
deeper_analysis: The drift pattern in Part 2b is structural and predictable: every count derived through template variables ({{CA_NONE}}, {{CA_HARDCODED}}, etc.) is correct because build.js recomputes on each build. Every count typed as a literal in sections.json prose is stale to some degree. The ratio of templated to literal numbers in §2b.1 is roughly 6:11 — the section has more hand-typed counts than computed ones in its narrative paragraphs, exactly inverting the discipline the project claims to follow ("never hardcode a number that can be derived from the data — we criticize the dome model for doing exactly that," CLAUDE.md "Computed Counts (never hardcode)" section). This is not a one-time drift; it is a process gap. The same gap allowed ISS-2216 (template glitch on derive-count) to land 24 hours before this review. Recommended structural mitigation: add a build-script linter that scans sections.json for digit-pattern sentences ("[0-9]+ are", "[0-9]+ of [0-9]+", "the [0-9]+ ...") that occur outside template variable substitution, and either flag them for review or require an inline-comment opt-out ("hardcoded-by-design: <reason>"). This converts the failure mode from silent staleness into a build-time signal.

citations_verified: data/wins.json:043 baseWins counts (CA_NONE_by_verdict sum = 43, matches templated {{CA_NONE}})
data/sections.json:part2b.html §2b.1 stratification paragraph hardcoded numbers (2/7/15/50/3/2 verbatim)
data/sections.json:part2b.html §2b.1 contrast paragraph "14 self-contradicted WINs" verbatim
citations_unchecked: raw-text/monitor.py — not present in current snapshot; "39 monitoring domains" claim anchored to commit 8e4bf9b cannot be re-verified this run

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender reading Part 2b §2b.1 could mount this attack: "The reviewers say I do not compute my accuracy figure from data — they accuse my homepage of being hand-typed plain HTML in a .score-number class. Look at their own section 2b.1. They say 43 WINs have no monitoring code and then stratify: 2 + 7 + 15 + 3 + 2 = 29. Where are the other 14? They list WIN-067 as having no monitoring, then five paragraphs later cite it as the canonical example of a hardcoded monitoring domain producing the wrong answer. Their fifteen-misleading is actually ten. Their three-refuted is actually eight. Their fourteen-self-contradicted is actually twenty. Their other-seven hardcoded list is missing one entry. This is the same anti-pattern they accuse me of — and theirs is worse because they have build automation right there in the same repository, and they chose not to use it for these specific paragraphs while using it for the surrounding ones. Either their methodology is sloppy, or they made deliberate framing choices to keep the numbers round. Either way, the page that calls out my sloppy counting is itself a sloppy counting page." This is a single-shot, easily-verified rebuttal — anyone can clone the repo, run a count, see the mismatch. It is the strongest single attack on Part 2b precisely because the structural argument underneath is correct; the defender does not need to refute the substance, just to discredit our discipline.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Land the major_rewrite recommended above before the dome author or any other reviewer notices the drift. Add the build-time invariant test as a defensive measure so this class of drift cannot recur silently. Consider adding a single-sentence "auditability footnote" at the top of Part 2b acknowledging that every numeric claim in this section is build-time-derived from data/wins.json and reproducible via `node test.js` — this preempts the "they cherry-pick numbers" attack by making the derivation auditable.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-067 is inconsistently classified across §2b.1 (line 13: implicitly monitoring=none) vs §2b.2 (line 24: silent-substitution, requires monitoring presence) vs §2b.4 (line 50: implicit QUAD_FAIL, satisfied with monitoring=hardcoded). WIN-034 is classified as "theological" in §2b.1 line 13 but as "Self-Contradicted" in current wins.json (verdict field) and in any other section that references it. WIN-023 — the genuine second Unfalsifiable CA_NONE WIN — does not appear in §2b.1 stratification even though it is the correct member of that bucket. WIN-058b is excluded from baseWins by the 3-digit-ID filter, so the §2b.1 prose claim "Two are not demonstrated" is referencing a WIN that the surrounding {{CA_NONE}}/{{CA_REVIEWED}} template values do not include — readers cannot see the WIN we are counting.

§2b.1 "Seven are self-contradicted by the dome's own geometry" — number 7 wrong; actual 12 CA_NONE Self-Contradicted; listed IDs include 067 (hardcoded, not none) and exclude 034/047/048/052/054/055
§2b.1 "50 are standard-physics relabellings" — number 50 wrong; actual relabels_standard=true within CA_NONE is 28; within all reviewed is 49
§2b.1 "Three are refuted by published data" — number 3 wrong; actual 8
§2b.1 "Two are not demonstrated" — number 2 wrong; actual 0 within baseWins (058b excluded by ID filter)
§2b.1 contrast paragraph "the dome's 14 self-contradicted WINs" — number 14 wrong; actual 20
§2b.1 contrast paragraph "seven have no monitoring at all" — number 7 wrong; actual 12
§2b.1 contrast paragraph "the other seven (WIN-002/012/029/038/045/046/049)" — number 7 wrong; actual 8; missing WIN-067

§2b.4 line 50 "every tidal constituent (WIN-045/046/049/050/051)" — verified: under build.js QUAD_FAIL definition (monitoring∈{hardcoded,none} AND relabels AND posthoc AND !derives_from_dome) all 5 IDs satisfy → "every" holds

§2b.4 line 50 "every Schumann resonance claim (WIN-001/002/029/038/061)" — verified: all 5 satisfy build.js QUAD_FAIL → "every" holds (NB: a previous reading classified this hole as overstating; the build.js definition rescues it)
quantitative_verification.computations: All counts computed via node -e against /sessions/admiring-kind-babbage/dome-curmudgeon-clone/data/wins.json with baseWins = /^\d{3}$/.test(id). CA_NONE_by_verdict: {Misleading:10, Refuted:8, Self-Contradicted:12, Std Model Explains:11, Unfalsifiable:2}, sum 43 = templated {{CA_NONE}}. QUAD_FAIL computed under build.js definition (generate-html.js:1217-1229): 47 IDs including 001/050/051/061/067.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Six numeric assertions in §2b.1 do not match wins.json. Three WIN-ID list assertions are incomplete or contain misclassifications. The §2b.4 "every tidal/every Schumann" claims are correct under build.js definition (preserved after re-checking) but rely on a definition where CA_QUAD_FAIL includes monitoring=none WINs; if a reader assumes QUAD_FAIL requires hardcoded monitoring, the "every" claim looks self-contradicting against §2b.1 line 13. This is a definition-clarity gap, not a numeric one.
